title: Audit Medication Review Dosage and Individual Confirmation champion code against champion guidelines
description: Compare the published champion guidelines and current live champion implementations for Medication Review: Dosage and Medication Review: Individual Confirmation. Record concrete inconsistencies, conflicts, translation gaps, and risky ambiguities in decision logic, scope, enrollment-type handling, thresholds, exclusions, ambiguity handling, and evidence standards. No edits in this task; audit only.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting audit. Plan: identify published champion guidelines for Medication Review: Dosage and Individual Confirmation, inspect current champion score implementations, compare scope/thresholds/exclusions/ambiguity handling/evidence standards, and return findings only. No edits.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Audit complete. Key findings:

- Dosage live champion code scores against every medication discussed rather than current medications only, and omits the rubric exclusions for no-longer-taken / discontinued / short-course meds plus the rule to treat unclear status as current.
- Dosage live champion code also omits the explicit schedule-only exclusion and treats any patient contradiction in SPM as No, while the rubric says unresolved contradiction only.
- Individual Confirmation live champion code lacks the rubric's applicability-first bounded-segment logic, truncated/missing-context guardrails, anti-pooling rule, sparse-confirmation rule, uncertainty/caregiver-deferral rule, and several batch-pattern exclusions.
- Individual Confirmation live champion code is also materially tighter in one dimension: it frames Yes as pause-after-each-item back-and-forth, whereas the detailed rubric allows a predominant individualized pattern with salvage for a few missed or paired items.

Sources compared: local published/detailed rubric text in scorecard markdown / working-copy prompt versus pulled API champion YAML for the live score versions.
